summary:
Citrate-based anticoagulants are suitable for electrolyte estimation because they prevent clotting by binding calcium without interfering with ion concentrations or assay accuracy. This makes them ideal compared to other anticoagulants such as EDTA or heparin which can affect test results through interference or chemical reactions.

The correct answer is B — citrate.
